title: pilosella subcymigera subsp. parciflocciformis
intro: Pilosella subcymigera subsp. parciflocciformis, also known as a subspecies of the Pilosella subcymigera plant, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to certain regions of Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pilosella subcymigera subsp. parciflocciformis include treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, as well as being used to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from its flowers, leaves, or roots, although specific preparation methods may vary depending on the intended use.
